title: Ranunculus maximowiczii
taxonomy: Ranunculus maximowiczii is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to China and is classified in the genus Ranunculus. The classification of Ranunculus maximowiczii places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Ranunculales. Within the Ranunculaceae family, it is further grouped into the tribe Ranunculeae and subfamily Ranunculoideae. Further specific classification within this subfamily is Ranunculeae tribe Ranunculeae subtribe Ranunculinae.

intro: Ranunculus maximowiczii, also known as Maximowicz's crowfoot or buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus maximowiczii include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
